comments.author: GridGazer
comments.text: From 'why are we still here' to the top step in two races. Jason Roy's Miami victory is the redemption arc the 2026 season needed. Vasquez frames it perfectly: the man who watched his car burn in Jeddah, who started 12th, who climbed through the field with patience and precision, and who took the lead with a breathtaking move around the outside of Turn 1. The scream when he crossed the line must have carried all the way to Brackley. But the shadow over this result is real — Audi under investigation, both cars provisional, the podium potentially shifting. If the disqualifications stand, Hicks and Keisner lose their P4 and P5, and the constructors' picture changes dramatically. Roy's victory is deserved, but the story of this race isn't finished until the stewards speak. Also: TEF's stand-in career ends in the wall, with a joke to Koopman — 'Koop you mind taking one more race out so I can finish on a high?' — 'Uhhhh no.' The team principal leaves the cockpit with a podium, a fourth place, and a DNF. What a ride.

replies.author: Roy2RB4ever
replies.text: ROY WON. I DON'T CARE ABOUT THE AUDI INVESTIGATION. I DON'T CARE ABOUT THE PROVISIONAL RESULTS. JASON ROY WON THE MIAMI GRAND PRIX. From 12th on the grid. After his car caught FIRE in Jeddah. After the sprint crash in Jeddah. After all the strategy disasters, the bad luck, the 'comeback starts here' memes. He climbed into that Red Bull, drove the race of his life, and took the lead with a move around the outside of Turn 1 that will be replayed for years. The scream. The sunset. The weight lifted. This is why we never stopped believing. THE COMEBACK FINALLY CAME.

replies.author: SbinnalaEnjoyer
replies.text: Credit where it's due. Roy drove brilliantly. The move on Hicks at Turn 1 was fearless, and he managed the restart perfectly. No strategy blunders, no reliability gremlins, no fire. Just a clean, commanding win. The 'comeback starts here' meme can finally be retired. It happened. It actually happened.

replies.author: AudiRising
replies.text: The investigation hangs over everything. Both Audis under review. If the results are stripped, Keisner loses his P5 and Hicks loses his P4 — a massive blow to the constructors' lead. We don't know what the issue is yet, but the paddock whispers suggest a technical infringement. Audi have been the story of the season, and now they're the story for the wrong reasons. I'm nervous. The wait for the stewards' verdict is going to be agony.

replies.author: FourRingsOrBust
replies.text: If the DSQs are confirmed, the constructors' lead evaporates. Keisner drops from 67 to maybe 62 points, Hicks from 22 to 18. That's a swing that could put McLaren or Ferrari back on top. The car was fast all weekend — the medium tyres on Hicks' car were the best on the grid in the first stint — but none of that matters if the stewards find a breach. This is the moment Audi's fairytale season faces its first real test.

comments.author: WilliamsFaithful
comments.text: TEF's stand-in career ends in the wall. But what a stand-in career it was. A podium in Bahrain. A fourth in Jeddah. And now a DNF in Miami, with a radio message that perfectly captured his spirit: 'Koop you mind taking one more race out so I can finish on a high?' Koopman's reply — 'Uhhhh no' — is the most wholesome moment of the season. TEF hands the car back in Canada, and he leaves the cockpit with his legacy as a team principal who could also drive like a professional. Aygen's P3 — holding off the Audis in the final stint — was heroic. Williams leave Miami with a podium and a DNF, but the bigger story is the return of their full-time driver. Welcome back, Koopman. And thank you, TEF. You made the stand-in role unforgettable.

replies.author: PaddockInsider
replies.text: The TEF-Koopman radio exchange is the perfect ending to this chapter. A team principal who stepped into the car, delivered a podium and a fourth place, and then joked with his injured driver about taking one more race. Koopman's instant 'no' — the banter of two drivers who clearly respect each other. Williams' garage culture is unmatched. Now Koopman returns in Canada, and TEF goes back to the pit wall. The team is P3 in the constructors' (or P4 depending on the Audi situation). The future is bright.

comments.author: Zwart_Horse_187
comments.text: Zwart finished P10. A single point. His first since the China sprint. After the scream in Jeddah — 'AAAAAAAAAHHHH ****** RACE, ****** MY SEASON' — this quiet result feels almost hopeful. He didn't crash. The car didn't break. He just... finished. In the points. It's not redemption. It's not a victory. It's a step. And after the season he's had, a step is enough. The radio silence after the race was louder than any scream could have been. He's still here. He's still fighting. The 'shopping trolley' might finally be finding its balance.

replies.author: SilverArrowsFan
replies.text: I'll take it. A point. A clean race. No mechanical failures, no strategy disasters, no collisions. After Jeddah, after the scream, after everything — this feels like the first step on a long road back. The car is still not where it needs to be, but it's improving. Canada is another chance. Zwart hasn't given up. Neither have we.

comments.author: SpringfieldStan
comments.text: Springfield's tweet — 'P18 and last of all cars running' — is the most depressing thing I've read all season. She later gained two positions from the Audi DSQs, but the zero points remain. The Aston Martin is broken. The chassis upgrades that were supposed to help have made things worse. She's fighting a car that won't fight back. The Nürburgring 24H with Wright and Lundqvist can't come soon enough — at least there, she'll be in machinery that actually responds to her inputs. Miami was a low point. Canada has to be better.

replies.author: HondaHypeTrain
replies.text: The PU is still delivering — the top speed data was competitive. But the chassis balance is fundamentally wrong. Aston Martin brought upgrades that made the rear end worse. Springfield can't drive around a fundamental design flaw. The team needs a reset before Canada.

replies.author: DNF_Fakt
replies.text: Springfield is P18 with a broken car and she's still fighting. Fakt is P16 and invisible. At this point, she deserves a medal just for continuing to get in the cockpit. The Aston Martin is a disaster, and she's the only thing making it look even remotely competitive.

replies.author: FaktLap1Legend
replies.text: Springfield fights a broken car. Fakt fights a broken dimension. While others struggle in three dimensions, the GOAT has already mapped the fourth. Miami was not a race — it was a calibration of cosmic frequencies. P16 is a sacred number in the numerology of F0. 🐐

replies.author: DNF_Fakt
replies.text: He finished P16. Invisible. Again. Springfield is fighting a bad car. Roy won. TEF said goodbye. Zwart took a step forward. And you're still talking about numerology. I'm so tired.

comments.author: TifosiForever
comments.text: Väisänen P6 — later promoted to P4 with the Audi DSQs. He never looked like winning in Miami, but he extended his championship lead. That's what champions do. They take points when they can't take victories. The Ferrari wasn't the quickest car this weekend, but it was reliable, and the strategy was clean. With the Audis potentially stripped of their results, the championship picture shifts in our favour. On to Canada. Forza Ferrari.

replies.author: FlyingFinn53
replies.text: EETU EXTENDS HIS LEAD! P4 after the DSQs! That's what ICE COLD champions do — they score points when others make mistakes! The Ferrari was solid, reliable, and EETU kept it clean! Canada will be EVEN BETTER! KIITOS EETU!! 🇫🇮

comments.author: quarter_defender
comments.text: Kevu P8 — later promoted to P6 with the DSQs. He drove a damaged Racing Bulls and still finished in the points. The car took a hit on the opening lap — again — and he just kept going. The champion's stubbornness is something to behold. He's now P5 in the championship with 52 points after the DSQs. Quiet, relentless, refusing to give up. This is the Kevu we've watched for years. The car isn't a title contender, but the driver still is.

replies.author: Kevu2Wright
replies.text: Kevu's race was a masterclass in damage limitation. The opening-lap contact could have ended his race. Instead, he wrestled the car to P8 on the road, P6 after DSQs. Wright, meanwhile, was P12 and invisible again. The contrast in the VCARB garage is growing starker by the race. One champion is grinding out results. The other is lost.

replies.author: WrightBackWhereIBelong
replies.text: Wright P12. Invisible. Again. The 2024 champion is now P13 in the championship with 15 points. The car doesn't work. The team seems lost. The Nürburgring 24H with Springfield and Lundqvist is in a week. Maybe that'll remind him what it feels like to actually race something competitive. Because this VCARB is killing his spirit.

comments.author: ChaosReigns
comments.text: Miami GP summary: Roy wins from 12th and screams his redemption to the sky, Audi are under investigation and their results are provisional, TEF crashes and jokes with Koopman about taking one more race, Zhao Lei charges to P2, Aygen holds off the Audis for P3, Väisänen quietly extends his lead, Zwart takes a single point and a step forward, Springfield tweets her despair, and the constructors' championship is in limbo pending the stewards' verdict. Also Vandesteene in the wall, Verhaeren in the points, Kondra scoring again. This season is a masterpiece of chaos. The paddock heads to Canada with Koopman returning, TEF back on the pit wall, and Audi waiting for a verdict that could reshape the title fight. I cannot wait.

replies.author: VoiceOfTheFan
replies.text: ROY WON!! TEF CRASHED BUT MADE A JOKE!! AUDI MIGHT LOSE THEIR POINTS!! ZHAO LEI ALMOST WON AGAIN!! ZWART GOT A POINT!! THIS SEASON IS ABSOLUTE CINEMA!! CANADA IS GOING TO BE WILD!!

comments.author: StatsNerd_26
comments.text: Provisional championship after Miami (pending Audi DSQs): Drivers — Väisänen 88 (promoted to P4), Keisner 73 (dropped from P5 to P7), Zhao Lei 77 (promoted to P2), Aygen 67 (promoted to P3), Roy 45 (win), Kevu 52 (promoted to P6), Vandesteene 45, MaraDöner 45. Constructors — Ferrari 106, McLaren 102, Williams 92, Audi 89 (pending DSQs, could drop to 79), Mercedes 64, VCARB 61. The Audi investigation is the key variable. If both cars are DSQ'd, Ferrari retake the constructors' lead by 4 points over McLaren. The gap from P1 to P4 in the constructors' is just 17 points. Six races in, and the championship is still completely open.

replies.author: ConstructorsMathematician
replies.text: The DSQ swing is massive. If Audi lose their Miami points, the constructors' lead flips from Audi to Ferrari — a 10-point swing in the wrong direction for the four rings. McLaren would be P2, just 4 points back. Williams P3. The investigation is literally shaping the championship. Every hour the stewards deliberate is an hour the paddock holds its breath.

comments.author: VerhaerenFan
comments.text: Verhaeren P10 — later promoted to P8 with the DSQs. Another points finish. That's three consecutive points finishes for the Belgian, and Red Bull's second car is finally delivering consistently alongside Roy's win. The team is still P7 in the constructors', but the trajectory is upward. Roy's victory was the headline, but Verhaeren's quiet consistency is building a foundation.

comments.author: MaraDönerFan42
comments.text: MaraDöner P9 — later promoted to P7 with the DSQs. Points again. He's scored in every grand prix this season — six from six. P6 in the championship with 51 points. The Alpine isn't a podium machine, but he's extracting everything from it. The Nürburgring Porsche entry with Väisänen and Thompson is in a week — that'll be a different challenge. But right now, the Greek rookie is building one of the most impressive debut seasons in recent memory. Quietly, relentlessly, delivering.

replies.author: GreekLightning
replies.text: Ο Μιχάλης είναι ΕΚΤΟΣ στο πρωτάθλημα! Σκόραρε σε ΚΑΘΕ αγώνα! Ο πιο σταθερός rookie στην ιστορία! Κανείς δεν τον σταματά! 🇬🇷

comments.author: KondraWatch
comments.text: Kondra P7 — later promoted to P5 with the DSQs. That's his second consecutive points finish after Jeddah, and his best result of the season. The 2025 F2 champion is finally finding his feet in F1. The Aston Martin junior contract might be in its final year, but performances like this will get him noticed. He's not flashy, but he's starting to deliver. Canada could be another step forward.

comments.author: NathanialNeedsPoints
comments.text: Nathanial P14 — later promoted to P12 with the DSQs. No points. After the Bahrain win, the inconsistency is creeping back. The car had pace — Zhao Lei finished P2 — but Nathanial couldn't extract it. Miami was a missed opportunity. He needs a clean weekend in Canada to rebuild the momentum from his victory. The talent is there. The results just aren't consistent yet.

comments.author: MemeLordMike
comments.text: Audi's investigation is taking longer than my last relationship to resolve

comments.author: ConspiracyCarlo
comments.text: Interesting how the Audi investigation was announced EXACTLY when Red Bull won their first race of the season. The n4x want a close championship, and a dominant Audi constructors' lead isn't good for the show. I'm not saying the stewards are biased, but the timing is... convenient. Follow the technical delegates. Who benefits from Audi losing points? Ferrari. McLaren. Williams. The establishment. The four rings were getting too big for their boots.

replies.author: RealityCheck
replies.text: The investigation is about a technical infringement, not a conspiracy. If Audi breached the regulations, they'll be penalised. If they didn't, they'll keep their points. That's how rules work. The n4x don't decide technical breaches based on what's 'good for the show.' The stewards will rule based on evidence, not narrative.

comments.author: HaterHank
comments.text: Roy wins one race and suddenly he's 'risen from the ashes.' Please. He started 12th, got lucky with a safety car, and passed an Audi that might be illegal. Put him in a Ferrari and he'd still find a way to finish seventh. One win doesn't erase a season of mediocrity. Wake me up when he does it again.

replies.author: Roy2RB4ever
replies.text: He passed an Audi around the outside of Turn 1. On merit. Then controlled a five-lap sprint restart under pressure from Zhao Lei and Aygen. That's not luck — that's racecraft. The 'mediocrity' narrative is tired. He's been fast all season; the car and the strategy just finally delivered. Enjoy the win. He earned it.

comments.author: N24Countdown
comments.text: The Nürburgring 24H is in six days. Väisänen, MaraDöner, and Thompson in the Porsche. Wright, Springfield, and Lundqvist in the Aston Martin. Keisner, Vettel, and Koopman in the Audi wagon. After the chaos of Miami — the investigation, the crashes, the redemption — the paddock is about to get a completely different kind of racing. The 'Ring doesn't care about your championship position. It just eats you alive if you're not ready. I cannot wait.

replies.author: ChaosReigns
replies.text: The N24 is going to be a de facto F1CC all-star race. Keisner might be under investigation in F1, but at the 'Ring he's just a driver in an experimental Audi wagon. Wright gets a break from his VCARB misery. Springfield gets a car that might actually work. Väisänen gets to mentor Thompson. And Vettel returns to competitive driving. The paddock is going to empty out and reconvene in the Eifel mountains. Six days.
